parts.content: What should I do if I receive an error indicating that a dataset is still in use when running terraform destroy?

content.question: Terraform: Teardown of BigQuery Dataset
content.content:
When running `terraform destroy`, the following error can occur:

```
Do you really want to destroy all resources?

Terraform will destroy all your managed infrastructure, as shown above.

There is no undo. Only 'yes' will be accepted to confirm.

Enter a value: yes

google_bigquery_dataset.homework_dataset: Destroying... [id=projects/terraform-demo-449214/datasets/homework_dataset]

╷

│ Error: Error when reading or editing Dataset: googleapi: Error 400: Dataset terraform-demo-449214:homework_dataset is still in use, resourceInUse
```

This is because the dataset is still in use by a table. To delete the dataset, set the `delete_contents_on_destroy` property to `true` in the `main.tf` file.

content.question: Terraform: Terraform initialized in an empty directory! The directory has no Terraform configuration files. You may begin working with Terraform immediately by creating Terraform configuration files.
content.content:
This error occurs when `terraform init` is run outside the working directory.

To resolve this issue:

1. Navigate to the working directory that contains your Terraform configuration files.
2. Run the `terraform init` command inside the correct directory.

Make sure your configuration files (e.g., .tf files) are present in the directory before running the command.

content.question: Terraform: Install for WSL
content.content: You can configure Terraform on Windows 10 using the Linux Subsystem (WSL) by following this guide: [Configuring Terraform on Windows 10 Linux Subsystem](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/azure-developer-community-blog/configuring-terraform-on-windows-10-linux-sub-system/ba-p/393845).

parts.content:
If you encounter an error indicating that a dataset is still in use when running `terraform destroy`, it typically means the dataset is being referenced by another resource, such as a table. To resolve this issue, you can modify your Terraform configuration.

You should set the `delete_contents_on_destroy` property to `true` in your `main.tf` file. This change allows Terraform to delete all contents (tables or views) within the dataset before removing the dataset itself.

Here's a brief example of how you could adjust your dataset resource definition:

```hcl
resource "google_bigquery_dataset" "homework_dataset" {
  dataset_id = "homework_dataset"
  location   = "US"
  delete_contents_on_destroy = true
}
```

After making this change, you can try running `terraform destroy` again.

You can read more about this error and solution in the [Terraform: Teardown of BigQuery Dataset](https://github.com/DataTalksClub/faq/blob/main/_questions/data-engineering-zoomcamp/module-1/126_9f9a1b9e4f_terraform-teardown-of-bigquery-dataset.md) document.
